widgets.description: This metric indicates the uptime of the running Redis server process.

filter.expression: project_id = $project_id AND instance_id in $instance_id AND gcp.resource_type = 'redis_instance'

widgets.title: Server Uptime

widgets.description: This metric shows the current memory usage of an instance that's expressed as a ratio of its maximum memory limit.

filter.expression: project_id = $project_id AND instance_id in $instance_id AND gcp.resource_type = 'redis_instance'

widgets.title: Memory Usage Ratio

widgets.description: This metric represents the amount of memory that's in use, expressed as a ratio of the total memory that's available for the system.

filter.expression: project_id = $project_id AND instance_id in $instance_id AND gcp.resource_type = 'redis_instance'

widgets.title: System Memory Usage Ratio

widgets.description: % CPU utilisation calculated by rate at which CPU-seconds are consumed by the Redis server main thread, broken down by system/user space.

filter.expression: project_id = $project_id AND instance_id in $instance_id AND gcp.resource_type = 'redis_instance'

widgets.title: % Main Thread CPU Utilisation

widgets.description: This metric displays the number of clients that are connected to an instance.

filter.expression: project_id = $project_id AND instance_id in $instance_id AND gcp.resource_type = 'redis_instance'

widgets.title: Connected Clients

widgets.description: This metric shows the ratio of successful Redis database calls (hits) to the total number of calls (hits plus misses)

filter.expression: project_id = $project_id AND instance_id in $instance_id AND gcp.resource_type = 'redis_instance'

widgets.title: Cache Hit ratio

widgets.description: This metric shows the ratio of successful Redis database calls (hits) to the total number of calls (hits plus misses)

filter.expression: project_id = $project_id AND instance_id in $instance_id AND gcp.resource_type = 'redis_instance'

widgets.title: Number of Keys

widgets.description: This metric represents the average time spent for each Redis command call over one minute. This time is measured in microseconds.

filter.expression: project_id = $project_id AND instance_id in $instance_id AND gcp.resource_type = 'redis_instance'

widgets.title: Time per Call

widgets.description: This metric measures the number of Redis commands that are run each minute on a primary instance. These include all open source Redis commands, including SET , GET, and HSET .

filter.expression: project_id = $project_id AND instance_id in $instance_id AND gcp.resource_type = 'redis_instance'

widgets.title: Number of Calls

widgets.description: This metric tracks the number of client connections that are waiting for data to become available because blocking commands are running in an instance.

filter.expression: project_id = $project_id AND instance_id in $instance_id AND gcp.resource_type = 'redis_instance'

widgets.title: Blocked Clients

widgets.description:
This metric tracks the number of connection attempts that an instance rejects because of the following reasons:

The maxclients limit: the server reaches the maximum allowed number of concurrent client connections.
Memory pressure: the instance rejects new connections because of high memory utilization.

filter.expression: project_id = $project_id AND instance_id in $instance_id AND gcp.resource_type = 'redis_instance'

widgets.title: Rejected Connections
